game_name: Super Street Fighter II: The New Challengers

danger_signals.visual_form: character portrait becomes bruised/bloodied in beat-up state
danger_signals.meaning: opponent health is critically low; immediate threat of defeat

danger_signals.visual_form: on-screen combo number display appears and increments
danger_signals.meaning: opponent is in vulnerable hit-stun state; continued assault possible

danger_signals.visual_form: character enters distinctive pose/animation with visual effects (e.g., Hadouken projectile)
danger_signals.meaning: high-damage attack incoming; requires evasion or blocking

danger_signals.visual_form: characters occupy close screen space with reduced distance
danger_signals.meaning: immediate threat range; throw or close-range attack risk

safety_signals.visual_form: opponent character defeated animation; score/round display updates
safety_signals.meaning: threat eliminated for this round; temporary safety state

safety_signals.visual_form: characters positioned far apart on screen
safety_signals.meaning: recovery window; opportunity to plan next action without immediate pressure
active damage mitigation; player has tactical control

faction_indicators.color_identity: character-specific primary colors (Ryu: white/red, Ken: white/red, Blanka: green, Guile: blue/military khaki)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: character nationality/fighting tradition emblem (Ryu/Ken: Japan karate gi; Guile: US military insignia; Zangief: Soviet hammer-and-sickle aesthetic)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: stage backgrounds reflect character origin countries (Japan for Ryu/Ken, America for Guile, Russia for Zangief, etc.)

faction_indicators.color_identity: distinct color schemes: Cammy (green/blonde military), Fei Long (red/gold martial artist), T. Hawk (tan/brown indigenous), Dee Jay (bright colors/jamaican aesthetic)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cammy: Delta Red military insignia; Fei Long: Hong Kong action star elegance; T. Hawk: indigenous/tribal markers; Dee Jay: kickboxer/musician styling
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: stage backgrounds: Cammy (UK military), Fei Long (Hong Kong cityscape), T. Hawk (Mexican/ancestral landscape), Dee Jay (Jamaica tropical setting)

faction_indicators.color_identity: Balrog: red/black (brutish power); Vega: purple/white (refined evil); Sagat: red/gold (regal authority); M. Bison: purple/black (dark overlord)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: M. Bison: Shadaloo organization mark; Sagat: Thai royalty aesthetic; Vega: Spanish noble styling; Balrog: boxer championship aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: boss characters feature more elaborate animations and special move effects than regular fighters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: final stage (M. Bison's throne room in Shadaloo fortress) represents faction power base

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: opponent selection screen shows remaining fighters; tournament bracket implied through sequential matches
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: earlier opponents execute simpler, more predictable move patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: final boss opponents (Grand Masters) display enhanced visual effects and more elaborate stage environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Grand Masters execute complex combos, unpredictable move selection, and advanced AI patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: character sprite appears unmarked, clean, full animation frames available
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: character moves with speed and fluidity; all special moves accessible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: character portrait shows progressive damage (bruises, blood per source limitation); sprite may show visual damage state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: character animation may show injured movements; risk of knockout increases with each hit

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: round indicator updates; defeated character removed or plays knockout animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: remaining health carries to next round; psychological pressure manifests as more aggressive or defensive AI

environmental_storytelling.example: Ryu's stage features Japanese temple architecture and aesthetic; Guile's stage shows USA military base setting; Blanka's stage depicts Brazilian jungle
Cammy wears Delta Red military outfit signaling special forces faction; Fei Long wears kung fu movie star clothing; T. Hawk wears indigenous warrior garb reflecting ancestral connection theme
stages contain regional props (sumo ring for Sumo stage, Spanish castle for Vega's stage, jungle temple for Blanka) that reinforce world geography and character origin
character victory poses reflect their fighting style and personality (Ryu's meditative stance, Zangief's strength display, Cammy's military discipline)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: balanced move execution; seeks worthy opponents; pursues self-improvement theme
displays most complex move set; unpredictable attack patterns; psychological pressure tactics (taunts, complex spacing)
military precision in move execution; faster, technical move set reflecting special forces training
powerful grapple-focused moveset; targets represent Shadaloo (M. Bison's faction); ancestral justice motivation
serve M. Bison as lieutenants; display advanced AI before final confrontation with main boss
kickboxing-based moveset; thematic positioning as outsider seeking inspiration rather than revenge

notes: Super Street Fighter II on Genesis lacks explicit political/faction narrative compared to later entries, but uses visual design and character origin to communicate world geography and implicit power structures (Shadaloo as antagonist faction represented by M. Bison and lieutenants). New character introductions (especially Cammy and T. Hawk) hint at larger narrative conflict through design: Cammy's mysterious Shadaloo connection and T. Hawk's ancestral land theft establish world-state of oppression. The fighting game paradigm communicates danger/safety primarily through animation state, proximity, and health visualization rather than diegetic environmental hazards. Character color schemes and stage backgrounds serve as primary faction/nationality identifiers.
